commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: mirror-app
repository.description: English learning app through movie scenes

commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(deploy): use 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost in nginx healthchecks

Alpine Linux resolves localhost to IPv6 (::1), but nginx only listens
on IPv4. This caused healthchecks to fail, marking containers as
unhealthy. Traefik then filtered out unhealthy containers, resulting
in 404 errors on app.sleepcore.ru.

Changed healthcheck endpoints:
- mini-app/Dockerfile.prod: curl to 127.0.0.1:80
- docker-compose.prod.yml: mini-app and vk-mini-app wget to 127.0.0.1:80/health
- docker-compose.full.yml: vk-mini-app wget to 127.0.0.1:80/health

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add .nojekyll to stop Jekyll processing

Without this file GitHub Pages runs Jekyll on every push.
Jekyll was failing on emoji characters and complex JS content,
causing 'Page build failed' for the last 3 commits. Only the
first commit worked by luck before the content grew complex.

.nojekyll tells Pages to serve files as plain static assets.

commit.message:
fix: resolve golangci-lint Go version error + add release workflow

golangci-lint fix:
- Add 'go: "1.24"' to .golangci.yml to bypass version check
  (golangci-lint v1.64.8 built with Go 1.24 rejected go.mod's go 1.26.0)

New release workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml):
- Triggered manually via workflow_dispatch
- Inputs: version (e.g. v1.0.0), release_type (release/beta/alpha),
  make_latest (yes/no)
- Auto-adds 'v' prefix if missing, validates semver format
- Creates git tag automatically
- Builds Linux amd64 + arm64 binaries in parallel
- Builds & pushes Docker image (linux/amd64 + linux/arm64)
- Docker tags: vX.Y.Z, X.Y.Z, X.Y, X (+ latest if make_latest=true)
- Creates GitHub release (prerelease=true for alpha/beta)
- Generates checksums.txt for all binaries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Support workflow_dispatch with tag input for release uploads

Adds optional 'tag' input to workflow_dispatch so releases can be
triggered manually when tag push doesn't fire the workflow (GitHub
limitation with tags on existing commits). Both jobs resolve
RELEASE_TAG from either refs/tags/* or the input, and use it for
upload decisions.

Update Opus model to Opus 4.6

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ci): restore actions/setup-go for Windows with cache disabled

Go is not on default bash PATH on self-hosted runner. actions/setup-go
handles RUNNER_TOOL_CACHE lookup and PATH setup. On repeat runs it finds
the cached version and skips download. Disable cloud cache on Windows
since disk persists locally.

docs: actualizar hash en changelog #038

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Hospital Management Analytics System — full-stack Next.js 16 dashboard

Star Schema data model (Prisma + Neon PostgreSQL):
- Fact table: PatientEncounters with 7 indexes (FK + composite OLAP)
- Dimensions: Patients, Doctors, Departments, DateDimension (730-day calendar)
- Seed script: 1,200 encounters, 300 patients, 15 doctors, 10 departments

SQL optimization layer (CTE + window functions):
- Department Efficiency: AVG(wait_time) via CTE on composite index (dept_id, date_id)
- Revenue Trends: SUM() OVER + LAG() window functions for MoM growth
- Doctor Workload: heatmap matrix via GROUP BY doctor × day-of-week
- KPI aggregations: all 4 endpoints with sub-300ms query targets

Next.js 16 App Router dashboard:
- Recharts: Bar chart (dept efficiency), Area chart (revenue trends)
- Custom heatmap: doctor × weekday encounter matrix
- KPI cards: LOS, revenue, wait time, patient volume, procedures
- shadcn/ui components, Tailwind CSS v4, dynamic imports for SSR safety

Deployment configuration:
- lib/db.ts: Neon serverless adapter with singleton Prisma client
- next.config.ts: PPR, serverExternalPackages, security headers
- vercel.json: build pipeline with prisma generate pre-step

commit.message:
Filter out problems from disabled hosts

Issue: API returns problems from disabled hosts, but Zabbix web UI filters them out.
This causes Bob to report alerts that operators can't see in their UI.

Solution:
- Get host status when fetching trigger info (0=enabled, 1=disabled)
- Skip problems where host status != '0' (disabled)
- Only return problems from actively monitored hosts

This matches Zabbix web UI behavior and prevents confusion.

CODEMATCH: update status for recent parity fixes and underreported systems

Recent parity fixes (from pull):

- do.c: blocking --More-- stair ack (goto_level → docrt → WIN_MESSAGE)
- monmove.c: maybe_postmove_hideunder helper, pre-trapped m_move entry
- teleport.c: canonical flag constants, onscary probe fix, ported functions
- u_init.c: role-sensitive Caveman pet alignment context

Status upgrades for systems more complete than documented:
- pray.c:   [~] → [p]  (34/45 functions implemented)
- quest.c:  [~] → [a]  (19/22 functions implemented)
- region.c: [~] → [p]  (15 functions implemented)
- sit.c:    [~] → [a]  (dosit/rndcurse/attrcurse/take_gold implemented)
- steed.c:  [~] → [a]  (12 functions: mount/dismount/ride/kick/poly)
- vault.c:  [~] → [p]  (all 18 functions: full Croesus guard system)
- minion.c: [~] → [a]  (10 functions: demon hierarchy, msummon)

Summary: [a]:49→53, [p]:4→7, [~]:51→43

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(FFS-144): Save all 50 form fields in request creation API

POST /api/requests was only inserting 9 columns, silently discarding
property_type, access_notes, cat details, kitten info, feeding data,
urgency, and 30+ other fields from the form.

- Expand INSERT from 9 to 50 columns matching ops.requests schema
- Map initial_status → status (Quick Complete mode sets "completed")
- Map eartip_count → eartip_count_observed, feeding_schedule → feeding_frequency
- Return { success: true, status: "promoted" } so form auto-redirects

Fixes FFS-144
